title: Module 3 quiz: Subscriptions and recurring billing

questions.prompt: A team creates new Stripe Products every time pricing changes — ends up with hundreds of Products. Right structure?

choices.text: One Product per plan (Pro, Enterprise), multiple Prices per Product (monthly/yearly/currency variants). Products = abstract offerings; Prices = specific pricing. Lets pricing evolve without polluting the catalog.

questions.explanation: Products are the abstract offering; Prices are specific pricing options. Many Prices per Product is the canonical shape.

questions.prompt: A user upgrades to Premium but doesn't see Premium features until next month. Right fix?

choices.text: `proration_behavior: 'always_invoice'` when updating the subscription. Stripe bills the prorated difference immediately; customer gets features instantly. Align 'paid now' with 'features now.'

questions.explanation: Default `create_prorations` puts proration on next invoice. `always_invoice` bills immediately, matching feature access to payment.

questions.prompt: Why collect a card at trial start vs. at trial end?

choices.text: Trial-to-paid conversion is dramatically higher (often 2-3×) with card on file. Without it, every user must come back, find the page, enter card details. With card on file (via SetupIntent or subscription with trial), conversion is automatic.

questions.explanation: Card-at-trial-start is one of the highest-leverage subscription decisions. Mild signup friction beats much bigger trial-to-paid friction.

questions.prompt: Your subscription product has 38% recovery rate on failed payments — email-only dunning. Right upgrade?

choices.text: Multi-channel: email + in-app banner + reason-specific messaging + one-click Customer Portal link. Enable Stripe's free Card Account Updater. Recovery rates routinely jump to 60-70%. Highest-ROI churn lever on already-paying users.

questions.explanation: Multi-channel dunning + specific messaging + one-click fix + Card Account Updater roughly doubles recovery on most products.

questions.prompt: When is Stripe Customer Portal the right tool?

choices.text: For self-service management of EXISTING subscriptions (update card, change plan, cancel, view invoices). 30-minute setup; saves weeks of dev. Use Checkout for new sales. Portal is hosted by Stripe; branded with logo + color; not pixel-perfect but sufficient for 90% of needs.

questions.explanation: Portal handles the common 'manage my subscription' UX. Use Checkout for new sales; Portal for managing existing. Both Stripe-hosted, both save weeks of dev.
